title: D2 MCP HTTP + fab serve — Express app + per-session McpServer factory
description: Create packages/server/src/http.ts implementing createFabricHttpApp(port, projectRoot) returning an Express app that uses createMcpExpressApp from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/express. Implement per-session McpServer factory with JsonlEventStore for MCP resumability. Export startHttpServer() from packages/server/src/index.ts while preserving startStdioServer(). Add packages/cli/src/commands/serve.ts implementing 'fab serve' command with --port (default 7373) and --target flags. Port conflict detection with friendly EADDRINUSE error.

files.change: New file: import createMcpExpressApp from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/express; import StreamableHTTPServerTransport; implement per-session transports Map<string, StreamableHTTPServerTransport>; implement JsonlEventStore backed by .intent-ledger.jsonl for MCP resumability; createFabricHttpApp(port, projectRoot) returns Express app with POST /mcp handler using per-session createFabricServer() factory

Add export function startHttpServer(options: {port: number, projectRoot: string}): Promise<http.Server>; ensure startStdioServer() export is preserved and unmodified (stdio mode logs to stderr not stdout)

New file: Commander command 'serve'; options: --port <number> default 7373, --target <dir> (resolves via resolveDevMode from cli/src/dev-mode.ts); imports startHttpServer from @fenglimg/fabric-server; EADDRINUSE error handler outputs friendly message 'Port {port} in use — try --port {port+1}'; logs startup URL to stdout
Import and register the new serve command so it appears in fab --help

implementation: Create packages/server/src/http.ts: implement JsonlEventStore class with getEventsSince(lastEventId) reading .intent-ledger.jsonl; implement per-session transport factory; createFabricHttpApp() mounts createMcpExpressApp() which provides /mcp route with StreamableHTTPServerTransport, DNS rebinding protection (host:127.0.0.1), and session management

Update packages/server/src/index.ts: export startHttpServer(options) that calls createFabricHttpApp() and returns app.listen(port) as a Promise<Server>; preserve existing startStdioServer() export unchanged

Create packages/cli/src/commands/serve.ts: resolve projectRoot using resolveDevMode() from cli/src/dev-mode.ts (reuses existing target resolution logic); call startHttpServer; handle EADDRINUSE with process.on('uncaughtException'); print 'Fabric Dashboard: http://127.0.0.1:{port}' on successful start
Test: curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:7373/mcp with valid MCP JSON-RPC initialize request; verify mcp-session-id header in response

reference.pattern: Per-session McpServer factory + createMcpExpressApp SDK pattern
reference.examples: createFabricServer() at packages/server/src/index.ts:L23-34 is already a pure factory — use directly in per-session handler; resolveDevMode at packages/cli/src/dev-mode.ts:L30-56

criteria: curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:7373/mcp -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize",...}' returns valid MCP JSON-RPC response with mcp-session-id header
fab serve starts without error and prints 'Fabric Dashboard: http://127.0.0.1:7373'
stdio mode (fab mcp) still works unchanged — startStdioServer() unaffected
Occupying port 7373 and then running fab serve produces friendly EADDRINUSE error message suggesting alternative port

rationale.chosen_approach: Use SDK's createMcpExpressApp for zero-dependency HTTP MCP with built-in DNS rebinding protection; per-session factory is required by SDK contract
alternatives_considered: Stateless MCP mode (loses resumability via JsonlEventStore)
Separate HTTP process (unnecessary complexity for single-user local tool)
decision_factors: Zero new npm dependencies (Hono + Express already transitive in SDK v1.29)
SDK contract: connect() takes exclusive transport ownership
createFabricServer() already a pure factory — no refactoring needed
rationale.tradeoffs: Per-session factory creates a new McpServer per HTTP connection — acceptable for single-user local dashboard, not for high-concurrency scenarios

manual_checks: fab serve --target examples/werewolf-minigame-stub --port 7373 — verify startup message
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:7373/mcp with MCP initialize request — verify valid response
fab mcp (stdio mode) — verify still works independently
Occupy 7373 with nc -l 7373 then fab serve — verify friendly error message
success_metrics: MCP HTTP endpoint returns valid JSON-RPC response within 500ms
mcp-session-id header present in MCP response
stdio mode unaffected by HTTP changes
integration: @modelcontextprotocol/inspector can validate HTTP MCP endpoint behavior post-implementation

risks.description: StreamableHTTPServerTransport API differs between SDK minor versions
risks.mitigation: Pin @modelcontextprotocol/sdk version in package.json; read actual SDK .d.ts types before implementing
risks.fallback: Fall back to SSEServerTransport (deprecated but present) if StreamableHTTP unavailable in installed version
